seoTitle: How to Create and Generate a Report in ClickUp
metaDescription: Learn how to build a ClickUp Dashboard with charts and widgets, filter data by assignee or priority, and share polished reports with stakeholders.
intro: Turning raw task data into something you can actually present to stakeholders is where ClickUp's Dashboards Hub comes in. This guide covers creating a dashboard from a template or from scratch, adding interactive widgets like bar charts and pie charts, and filtering the data by assignee or custom field so your report tells the right story.

steps.heading: Open the Dashboards Hub
steps.body: From a project list, click '+ View' and select Dashboard from the app drawer.

steps.heading: Start from a template or blank canvas
steps.body: Choose a pre-built performance tracking template, or click Create Dashboard to start with a blank overview panel.

steps.heading: Review generated widgets
steps.body: Check the default pie chart distribution widget to audit workloads across your project's assignees.

steps.heading: Add a custom widget
steps.body: Click Add Card in the top toolbar to inject a new calculation element, such as a time-tracked total.

steps.heading: Filter for specific insights
steps.body: Customize your new widget's filters to isolate specific data, like overdue tasks only.

steps.heading: Share the report
steps.body: Use the Share option to configure client permissions or schedule an automated PDF email delivery of the dashboard.

faq.question: Can I generate a written summary instead of just charts?
faq.answer: Yes, a ClickUp Brain summary card can be added to automatically generate a written status insight report alongside your visual widgets.

faq.question: Can I share a dashboard with someone outside my team?
faq.answer: Yes, the Share option lets you configure external client permissions or schedule automated report delivery via email.

faq.question: What chart types are available for dashboards?
faq.answer: You can build bar charts, line graphs, and pie charts among other widget types, depending on how you want to visualize the data.

faq.question: Does the dashboard update automatically as tasks change?
faq.answer: Yes, dashboard widgets reflect live data from your connected lists, so they update as underlying tasks change.
